name: 光的折射
subject: physics
curriculum_points: 探究并了解光的反射定律。通过实验，了解光的折射现象及其特点。
通过光束从空气射入水（或玻璃）中的实验，了解光的折射现象及其特点。

excerpts.text: 探究并了解光的反射定律。通过实验，了解光的折射现象及其特点。
通过光束从空气射入水（或玻璃）中的实验，了解光的折射现象及其特点。

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 光的折射

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 物理
- **学段**: 初中
- **年级**: 八年级
- **章节**: 光学
- **知识点**: 光的折射
- **课标版本**: 义务教育课程标准（2022年版）

---

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
>
2.3.3 探究并了解光的反射定律。通过实验，了解光的折射现象及其特点。

例6 通过光束从空气射入水（或玻璃）中的实验，了解光的折射现象及其特点。
>
---
>
### 1.2 学业要求
>
同上
>
---
>
### 1.3 教学提示
>
同上
---

## 二、教学目标

### 2.1 知识与技能
- 学生能够理解光的折射的基本概念和原理
- 学生能够正确进行相关计算和推理
- 学生能够识别和描述相关的概念、现象或材料
- 学生能够理解光的折射的基本概念和原理

### 2.2 过程与方法
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习物理的方法
- 学生能够运用物理学科方法解决问题
- 学生能够在交流中表达自己的思路
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习物理的方法

### 2.3 情感态度与价值观
- 学生感受物理与现实生活密切相关
- 学生体验物理探究的乐趣，树立学好物理的信心
- 学生养成认真细致、独立思考的学习习惯
- 学生感受物理与现实生活密切相关

---

## 三、教学重难点

### 3.1 教学重点
- 光的折射的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构
- 光的折射的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构

### 3.2 教学难点
- 掌握光的折射的基本运算和简单应用
- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用
- 掌握光的折射的基本运算和简单应用
- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用

**知识点ID**: light-refraction
**状态**: gap

## 五、教学建议（学科化补灌）

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「光的折射」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“探究并了解光的反射定律。通过实验，了解光的折射现象及其特点。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

deep_textbook_snippets.text:
Medicine: Endoscopes A medical device called an endoscope is shown in Figure 16.24. Figure 16.24 Endoscopes, such as the one drawn here, send light down a flexible fiber optic tube, which sends images back to a doctor in charge of performing a medical procedure. The word endoscope means looking inside. Doctors use endoscopes to look inside hollow organs in the human body and inside body cavities. These devices are used to diagnose internal physical problems. Images may be transmitted to an eyepiece or sent to a video screen. Another channel is sometimes included to allow the use of small surgical instruments. Such surgical procedures include collecting biopsies for later testing, and removing polyps and other growths. Identify the process that allows light and images to travel through a tube that is not straight. a. The process is refraction of light. b. The process is dispersion of light. c. The process is total internal reflection of light. d. The process is polarization of light. Calculations with the Law of Refraction The calculation problems that follow require application of the following equations: 16.8 16.2 • Refraction
> **[Figure 300, Page 509]** A figure or diagram (487×310px) related to: fibers, thin, light, entering, likely, strike. This image appears on page 509 of the textbook.
![Figure 300 - A figure or diagram (487×310px) related to: fibers, thin, li](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_images/img_0300_c8551e7f.png)
and These are the equations for refractive index, the mathematical statement of the law of refraction (Snell’s law), and the equation for the critical

wave characteristics prominently. We call this part of optics geometric optics. This chapter focuses on situations for which geometric optics is suited. 16.1 Reflection Section Learning Objectives By the end of this section, you will be able to do the following: • Explain reflection from mirrors, describe image formation as a consequence of reflection from mirrors, apply ray diagrams to predict and interpret image and object locations, and describe applications of mirrors • Perform calculations based on the law of reflection and the equations for curved mirrors Section Key Terms angle of incidence angle of reflection central axis concave mirror convex mirror diffused focal length focal point geometric optics law of reflection law of refraction ray real image specular virtual image Characteristics of Mirrors There are three ways, as shown in Figure 16.2, in which light can travel from a source to another location. It can come directly from the source through empty space, such as from the Sun to Earth. Light can travel to an object through various media, such as air and glass. Light can also arrive at an object after being reflected, such as by a mirror. In all these cases, light is modeled as traveling in a straight line, called a ray. Light may change direction when it encounters the surface of a different material (such as a mirror) or when it passes from one material to another (such as when passing from air into glass). It then continues in a straight line—that is, as a ray. The word ray comes from mathematics. Here it means a straight line that originates from some poin

Medium n Zircon 1.923 Table 16.2 Indices of Refraction The table lists the indices of refraction for various materials that are transparent to light. Note, that light travels the slowest in the materials with the greatest indices of refraction. Figure 16.17 provides an analogy for and a description of how a ray of light changes direction when it passes from one medium to another. As in the previous section, the angles are measured relative to a perpendicular to the surface at the point where the light ray crosses it. The change in direction of the light ray depends on how the speed of light changes. The change in the speed of light is related to the indices of refraction of the media involved. In the situations shown in Figure 16.17, medium 2 has a greater index of refraction than medium 1. This difference in index of refraction means that the speed of light is less in medium 2 than in medium 1. Note that, in Figure 16.17(a), the path of the ray moves closer to the perpendicular when the ray slows down. Conversely, in Figure 16.17(b), the path of the ray moves away from the perpendicular when the ray speeds up. The path is exactly reversible. In both cases, you can imagine what happens by thinking about pushing a lawn mower from a footpath onto grass, and vice versa. Going from the footpath to grass, the right front wheel is slowed and pulled to the side as shown. This is the same change in direction for light when it goes from a fast medium to a slow one. When going from the grass to the footpath, the left front wheel moves faster than the others, and the mower changes dir

The amount that a light ray changes direction depends both on the incident angle and the amount that the speed changes. For a ray at a given incident angle, a large change in speed causes a large change in direction, and thus a large change in the angle of refraction. The exact mathematical relationship is the law of refraction, or Snell’s law, which is stated in equation form as In terms of speeds, Snell’s law becomes Here, n1 and n2 are the indices of refraction for media 1 and 2, respectively, and θ1 and θ2 are the angles between the rays and the perpendicular in the respective media 1 and 2, as shown in Figure 16.17. The incoming ray is called the incident ray and the outgoing ray is called the refracted ray. The associated angles are called the angle of incidence and the angle of refraction. Later, we apply Snell’s law to some practical situations. Dispersion is defined as the spreading of white light into the wavelengths of which it is composed. This happens because the index of refraction varies slightly with wavelength. Figure 16.18 shows how a prism disperses white light into the colors of the rainbow. 3. Explain your observations. Virtual Physics Bending Light Click to view content (https://www.openstax.org/l/28Bendinglight) The Bending Light simulation in allows you to show light refracting as it crosses the boundaries between various media (download animation first to view). It also shows the reflected ray. You can move the protractor to the point where the light meets the boundary and measure the angle of incidence, the angle of refraction, and the angle of ref

deep_worked_example.stem: 根据教材片段，设计一道关于「光的折射」的现象解释或实验探究题。
deep_worked_example.solution_outline: 先描述现象或实验条件，再指出关键概念和证据，最后给出解释或结论。
deep_worked_example.evidence_excerpt: Medicine: Endoscopes A medical device called an endoscope is shown in Figure 16.24. Figure 16.24 Endoscopes, such as the one drawn here, send light down a flexible fiber optic tube, which sends images back to a doctor in charge of performing a medical procedur

errors.description: 只套用「光的折射」相关公式，忽略物理情境、单位和适用条件。
实验分析时没有控制变量，导致无法判断因果关系。

exercises.stem: 围绕「光的折射」，写出一个可观察现象，并说明其中涉及的物理量或规律。
exercises.answer: 应扣住课标要点：探究并了解光的反射定律。通过实验，了解光的折射现象及其特点。，说明现象、条件和规律。

exercises.stem: 如果要验证「光的折射」相关规律，你会控制哪些变量？记录哪些数据？
exercises.answer: 需说明实验目的、控制变量、测量数据、可能误差和结论依据。

real_world: 观察家用电器、交通工具、运动器材或自然现象中与「光的折射」相关的物理规律。
